questions.question: Page numbers in Spring Data's Pageable are:
options: 1-indexed — first page is page 1
0-indexed — first page is page 0
Configurable — set spring.data.page-start in application.properties
Determined by the database driver being used

questions.question: What does Page<User>.map(UserResponse::from) do?
options: Converts Page<User> to a List<UserResponse>
Creates a new Page<UserResponse> with the same pagination metadata but content transformed to UserResponse
Creates a HashMap mapping User IDs to UserResponse objects
Applies the transformation lazily — entities are only converted when the client reads them

questions.question: The @PageableDefault(size = 20, sort = 'name') annotation does what?
options: Enforces a maximum page size of 20 — requests for more are rejected
Sets default values for page size and sort when the client doesn't specify them
Caches the first 20 results for faster subsequent requests
Applies globally to all repository methods in the application

questions.question: What SQL clause does Spring Data JPA generate for PageRequest.of(2, 10)?
options: LIMIT 10 OFFSET 10
LIMIT 10 OFFSET 20
FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY STARTING AT ROW 20
ROWNUM BETWEEN 21 AND 30

questions.question: A client calls GET /api/users. The response Page has totalPages: 5 and last: false. What does this mean?
options: There are 5 total users in the database
The current page has 5 items
There are 5 pages of results and there are more pages after the current one
The request timed out after 5 seconds, with a last-chance response
